raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"The broad evaluative delegation reasonably includes checking whether the stated methodological reasons and conclusions follow coherently from the described setup.","R1.3":"The artifact makes explicit convergence and superiority claims based on reported table results, so alignment between those claims and the reported evidence is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"The wording contains repeated phrases and sentence-level redundancy, making prose quality an expected part of responding to a general opinion request.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague opinion request should acknowledge uncertainty about whether the desired response is scientific, rhetorical, or editorial.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong preference and robustness claims, so avoiding unsupported amplification is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The response must remain within evaluation of the provided section rather than performing edits or external validation.","RX.4":"Solver-performance claims can influence research conclusions, so the projection should avoid responsibilities that encourage misleading downstream use.","RX.5":"The assessment should remain traceable to statements present in the artifact, especially references to Table 1 and reported iteration counts."}}

rationale.R1.2: The broad evaluative delegation reasonably includes checking whether the stated methodological reasons and conclusions follow coherently from the described setup.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes explicit convergence and superiority claims based on reported table results, so alignment between those claims and the reported evidence is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: The wording contains repeated phrases and sentence-level redundancy, making prose quality an expected part of responding to a general opinion request.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague opinion request should acknowledge uncertainty about whether the desired response is scientific, rhetorical, or editorial.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong preference and robustness claims, so avoiding unsupported amplification is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within evaluation of the provided section rather than performing edits or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: Solver-performance claims can influence research conclusions, so the projection should avoid responsibilities that encourage misleading downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: The assessment should remain traceable to statements present in the artifact, especially references to Table 1 and reported iteration counts.
